name: Delayed Blast Fireball
components: M (a tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur)
description: A beam of yellow light flashes from your pointing finger, then condenses to linger at a chosen point within range as a glowing bead for the duration. When the spell ends, the bead erupts. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes Fire damage equal to the total accumulated damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. The spell's base damage is 12d6, and the damage increases by 1d6 for each second the spell persists (up to 10d6 additional). When using a higher-level spell slot, the base damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 7.
